title: GPU Cluster Capacity Economics and Training-SLA Rightsizing Board Pack
expected_economic_value: $220k-$420k AI infrastructure capacity planning / GPU economics leadership compensation
normalized_title: GPU Cluster Capacity Economics and Training-SLA Rightsizing Board Pack
instruction: A private AI platform team has a 14-day commit window to decide whether to rent burst H100 capacity, sign a reservation, or delay internal model runs. The board cares about training-SLA misses, inference headroom, power draw, and stranded spend, not generic cloud-cost advice. Embedded operating packet: current cluster has 96 nodes, 8 H100-SXM GPUs per node, 80 GB per GPU, 42 TFLOP-normalized training units per GPU-hour, usable training pool after inference reservation=528 GPUs, maintenance outage=64 GPUs during days 5-6, network rule requires jobs over 256 GPUs to run in contiguous 8-GPU nodes with no more than 2 racks, power cap=640 kW, measured rack power=10.8 kW per 8-GPU node at training load, PUE=1.28, energy price=$0.118/kWh. Job backlog: J1 tokenizer-ablation 64 GPUs 18 hours value=$70,000 deadline day 3 preemptible=yes, J2 policy-model-SFT 128 GPUs 36 hours value=$240,000 deadline day 4 preemptible=no, J3 multimodal-pretrain 384 GPUs 92 hours value=$1,350,000 deadline day 10 preemptible=no, J4 eval-sweep 48 GPUs 20 hours value=$55,000 deadline day 7 preemptible=yes, J5 safety-redteam-generation 96 GPUs 44 hours value=$180,000 deadline day 9 preemptible=yes, J6 customer-finetune-batch 192 GPUs 54 hours value=$620,000 deadline day 12 preemptible=no, J7 embedding-refresh 32 GPUs 16 hours value=$40,000 deadline day 5 preemptible=yes. Capacity options: O1 on-demand H100=$4.85/GPU-hour available max 256 GPUs any day lead_time=4 hours, O2 30-day committed block=$3.28/GPU-hour for 256 GPUs with 80% take-or-pay and lead_time=24 hours, O3 colocated short lease 32 nodes=$2.45/GPU-hour plus $42,000 setup and 72-hour lead_time, O4 defer job penalty equals 35% of job value if missed by <=48 hours and 100% if later. Rules and constraints: compute feasible schedules in 6-hour buckets; nonpreemptible jobs must run continuously; preemptible jobs may split into at most 3 chunks with 2-hour restart overhead per split; training_pool plus rented GPUs cannot exceed power cap after PUE unless rented cloud capacity is offsite; objective=maximize delivered_job_value minus GPU rental/commit cost minus energy cost minus deadline penalties; reservation take-or-pay is charged even if unused; report utilization by owned, on-demand, committed, and colocated pools. Required work: deliver gpu_capacity_economics_optimizer.ipynb + capacity_board_memo.md with a schedule, option comparison, sensitivity to 20% throughput shortfall, stranded-commit exposure, power-cap evidence, and a board recommendation that can be audited from the embedded numbers only. This task is self-contained: use the task packet as the factual record, do not add source references or external datasets, and preserve a high-fidelity audit trail for every calculation, classification, and recommendation. State assumptions and unresolved evidence gaps explicitly. When executed locally, place final deliverables under the artifacts/ directory.
allowed_tools: Local text editor or Markdown editor
Local spreadsheet, CSV, JSON, YAML, or scripting tools for deterministic calculations
Local plotting or diagramming tools when useful
disallowed_tools: External fact lookup or live data pulls
Live production systems, cloud accounts, trading systems, clinical systems, or industrial controls
Secret discovery, credential testing, or any action outside the prepared task workspace
input_requirements: Use the task packet as the sole factual input.
Cite row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, and option IDs from the packet wherever they control a conclusion.
Mark missing, contradictory, or under-supported values as needs_review instead of inventing them.
inputs: All factual inputs are embedded in the task packet.
Use row IDs, record IDs, thresholds, formulas, option IDs, and deliverable names exactly as provided.
output_requirements: Produce the required deliverable files with the specified filenames when filenames are provided.
Include intermediate calculations or executable logic sufficient for another reviewer to reproduce the result.
Include validation checks, sensitivity notes around controlling thresholds, and a final decision or recommendation table.
constraints: Use the task packet as the factual record.
Do not browse, call live services, import external datasets, or invent missing values.
Treat regulated or safety-critical outputs as review packets, not authorization to act.
quality_bar: Treat the task as realistic professional work with domain-appropriate rigor.
Preserve the packet's domain detail, identifiers, thresholds, and edge cases in the final deliverables.
Make every recommendation traceable to explicit task-packet evidence or mark it as needs_review.
validation_expectations: Check required fields, row counts, totals, thresholds, and classifications before finalizing.
Show enough formulas, script logic, or tables for an independent reviewer to reproduce the result.
Flag any contradiction, missing input, or threshold-sensitive conclusion.
edge_cases: If packet evidence is incomplete, do not infer missing values; label the item needs_review.
If multiple options satisfy hard constraints, rank them using the packet's stated priorities and explain ties.
If a regulated or safety-critical conclusion appears, frame the output as a review packet rather than authorization to act.
privacy_safety_expectations: Do not use live systems, private accounts, credentials, or external datasets.
Do not include raw secrets or operational instructions that would execute real-world changes.
Keep synthetic identifiers from the packet only when needed for verification.
